figures.caption: Figure 2. The relative approximation error of ϕ̂ γ in approximating the Shapley value as γ varies.

Figure 3. The relative approximation error of SHAP-IQ, ϕ̂ γ and its adaptive variant. The adaptive method corresponds to Adalina, presented in Algorithm 1, which adaptively estimates the optimal γ. Weighted Banzhaf values are parameterized by w ∈ (0, 1), whereas Beta Shapley values are parameterized by (α, β), with (1, 1) corresponding to the Shapley value.

Figure 4. The relative approximation error of different randomized algorithms. Weighted Banzhaf values are parameterized by w ∈ (0, 1), whereas Beta Shapley values are parameterized by (α, β), with (1, 1) corresponding to the Shapley value.
Table 1. Summary of the datasets used.

Figure 5. The relative approximation error of different randomized algorithms. Weighted Banzhaf values are parameterized by w ∈ (0, 1), whereas Beta Shapley values are parameterized by (α, β), with (1, 1) corresponding to the Shapley value.
Figure 1. The relative approximation error of unbiased kernelSHAP in approximating the Shapley value with different sampling distributions. Here, λ = u[n]−u∅
